steps.title: Distribute and combine like terms
steps.content: Note $x^2y-10xy=xy(x-10)$, so the second fraction's denominator shares $(x-10)$ with the first. Get the common denominator $x^2y-10xy$ and combine:$$\frac{(y+13)\cdot xy}{x^2y-10xy}+\frac{y(x-10)}{x^2y-10xy}=\frac{xy^2+13xy+xy-10y}{x^2y-10xy}=\frac{xy^2+14xy-10y}{x^2y-10xy}$$

steps.title: Match to the answer choices
steps.content: The simplified expression is $\dfrac{xy^2+14xy-10y}{x^2y-10xy}$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
